cards.back: A simple sorting algorithm that repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements, and swaps them if they are in the wrong order.
A sorting algorithm that sorts an array by repeatedly finding the minimum element from the unsorted part and putting it at the beginning. The algorithm maintains two subarrays in a given array.

A simple sorting algorithm that builds the final sorted array (or list) one item at a time. It is much less efficient on large lists of data than more advanced algorithms like quicksort, heapsort, or merge sort.

cards.front: What is the time complexity of Bubble Sort?
cards.back: The best-case time complexity is O(n) and the worst-case time complexity is O(n^2).

cards.front: What is the time complexity of Selection Sort?
cards.back: The best-case time complexity is O(n^2) and the average-case time complexity is O(n^2). The worst-case time complexity is O(n^2).
